seoTitle: Record Credit Card Payments in QuickBooks Online
metaDescription: How to log a payment made toward your business credit card balance in QuickBooks Online using the Pay Down Credit Card feature.
intro: Paying off your business credit card balance needs to be recorded correctly so your checking account and credit card liability both reconcile. QuickBooks Online has a dedicated Pay Down Credit Card form for exactly this transfer. This guide covers logging the payment and matching it to your bank feed.

steps.heading: Open the payment form
steps.body: Click + New, then select Pay Down Credit Card under the Other column.

steps.heading: Choose the credit card account
steps.body: Select the specific credit card account you're paying down from the first dropdown.

steps.heading: Enter amount and date
steps.body: Type the exact amount transferred and set the payment processing date.

steps.heading: Select your funding source
steps.body: Choose your main checking account in the field asking what account you used to make the payment.

steps.heading: Add a memo and save
steps.body: Note the transaction in the memo box for a clean audit trail, click Save and Close, then verify it against your bank feed on the Bank Transactions screen.

faq.question: What's the Pay Down Credit Card form for specifically?
faq.answer: It's designed to record the transfer of funds from a checking account to pay off a credit card liability, keeping both ledgers accurate.

faq.question: Can I attach a check number to this kind of payment?
faq.answer: Yes, if you paid by paper check, there's a checkbox to record the associated check number within the same form.

faq.question: How do I confirm the payment matches my bank feed?
faq.answer: Go to Bank Transactions after saving to check that your recorded entry lines up with the corresponding bank feed line.

faq.question: Where can I see my current credit card liability balance?
faq.answer: The global Chart of Accounts dashboard shows your active revolving liabilities, including the current credit card balance.
